senses.definition: a medical condition involving inflammation of the uterus connected with excess mucus or mucus-like discharge
examples: The doctor used medical terminology to describe blennometritis after examining the symptoms.
Her report mentioned blennometritis as a possible cause of inflammation.

senses.definition: a medical term referring to inflammation associated with mucus
examples: The term blennophlogisma appeared in the medical notes.
The clinician suspected a mucus-related inflammatory condition.

senses.definition: a medical condition in which mucus is involved in inflammation
examples: The report listed blennophlogosis among the possible inflammatory causes.
Mucus and irritation were described as part of the inflammation.

senses.definition: an irrational fear of mucus or slime
examples: She has blennophobia, so she avoids places where she might see slime.
Even small amounts of mucus trigger strong anxiety for people with blennophobia.

senses.definition: inflammation of the eye connected with mucus or mucus-like discharge (a medical eye condition)
examples: The ophthalmology exam mentioned blennophthalmia as a possible diagnosis.
Mucus discharge from the eye was one of the signs described.

senses.definition: a medical condition involving coughing up mucus (mucus from the respiratory tract)
examples: Doctors considered blennoptysis when she reported mucus in her cough.
The symptoms included frequent coughing with mucus.

senses.definition: an excessive discharge of watery mucus, especially from the urethra or vagina; also used for abnormal eye discharge
examples: The term blennorrhea appeared in the medical text about abnormal discharge.
She was treated for symptoms described as blennorrhea.

senses.definition: medical bleeding or discharge connected with mucus, often referring to mucus-like flow with blood
examples: Her diagnosis list included blennorrhagia.
The clinician explained that blennorrhagia describes a mucus-related problem with bleeding or bloody discharge.

senses.definition: describing a condition involving mucus-related discharge, often with bleeding
examples: The doctor used the term blennorrhagic to describe the patient’s discharge.
The report listed blennorrhagic symptoms alongside other findings.

senses.definition: an excessive discharge of watery mucus, especially from the urethra or vagina; also used for abnormal eye discharge
examples: The doctor documented blennorrhea in the chart.
Blennorrhea can be described as a strong mucus discharge in medical texts.

senses.definition: relating to or producing blennorrhea, meaning producing excessive watery mucus discharge
examples: They described the condition as blennorrheal.
The symptoms were considered blennorrheal based on the type of discharge.

senses.definition: plural of blennorrhea: multiple cases of excessive watery mucus discharge
examples: The textbook listed several blennorrheas as examples of mucus discharge disorders.
Different blennorrheas may appear in different body areas.

senses.definition: a medical condition involving mucus-like discharge from the nose (a nasal mucus discharge problem)
examples: The ENT specialist used the term blennorrhinia to describe nasal mucus.
She reported symptoms that matched blennorrhinia.

senses.definition: an excessive discharge of watery mucus; used medically for abnormal discharge from the urethra or vagina, and also in ophthalmology for eye discharge
examples: The spelling blennorrhoea appears in some medical writings.
Her symptoms were described using the term blennorrhoea.

senses.definition: plural of blennorrhoea: multiple cases of excessive watery mucus discharge
examples: The article compared different blennorrhoeas.
Medical classifications may include several blennorrhoeas.

senses.definition: a medical state related to mucus (often involving abnormal mucus production or discharge)
examples: In the medical chapter, blennosis was described as a mucus-related condition.
Her symptoms were discussed in terms of possible blennosis.

senses.definition: a medical condition in which mucus is prevented from flowing properly and becomes stuck or stagnant
examples: Blennostasis was mentioned as a possible cause of blockage symptoms.
The doctor explained that mucus could build up in a state like blennostasis.

senses.definition: relating to blennostasis, meaning involving mucus being stopped or not flowing
examples: The treatment aimed to counter blennostatic effects.
The report described a blennostatic phase of mucus buildup.

senses.definition: a medical term for accumulation of mucus in the chest area, especially in the airways
examples: The clinician referenced blennothorax when discussing mucus in the lungs.
Her symptoms suggested mucus collection in the thoracic passages.

senses.definition: a medical term for an outflow or discharge of mucus
examples: The term blennotorrhea appeared in the discussion of discharge symptoms.
The doctor described an ongoing mucus discharge as blennotorrhea.

senses.definition: a medical condition involving mucus in the urine (urine with mucus-like material)
examples: Her urine test suggested blennuria.
The doctor explained that blennuria means mucus in urine.

senses.definition: a small marine fish, often with a long body and comb-like teeth
examples: We saw a blenny hiding in the rocks at low tide.
A blenny is a common type of small sea fish in shallow water.

senses.definition: a medical term for inflammation of mucus-secreting glands
examples: The term blennymenitis was used in the medical description.
The symptoms were linked to mucus-secreting gland inflammation.

senses.definition: a nonstandard or rare form that may refer to a mucus or slime-related term in medical word-building
examples: The word blens appeared as part of a larger medical term.
In her vocabulary list, blens was grouped with -blenn- words.

senses.definition: archaic: past tense of blend (to mix together)
examples: The poet wrote that colors were blent into one shade.
In the old story, their voices were blent into harmony.

senses.definition: archaic: mixed or blended together
examples: The manuscript described blent flavors from many places.
She used the blent ingredients as the recipe’s base.
